title: Gurdwara Jyoti Sarup
tagline: Gurdwara Jyoti Sarup — heritage city of Punjab

overview.short: Fatehgarh Sahib (Punjabi pronunciation: [fə.t̪éː.gə́ɽᵊ sä́ːbᵊ]) is a city and a sacred pilgrimage site of Sikhism in the north west Indian state of Punjab.
overview.description: Fatehgarh Sahib (Punjabi pronunciation: [fə.t̪éː.gə́ɽᵊ sä́ːbᵊ]) is a city and a sacred pilgrimage site of Sikhism in the north west Indian state of Punjab. It is the headquarters of Fatehgarh Sahib district, located about 5 kilometres (3.1 mi) north of Sirhind. Fatehgarh Sahib is named after Fateh Singh, the 7-year-old son of Guru Gobind Singh, who was seized and buried alive, along with his 9-year-old brother Zoravar Singh, by the Mughals on the orders of governor Wazir Khan during the ongoing Mughal-Sikh wars of the early 18th century. howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Khanda Museum
topPlaces.description: Khanda Museum is a museum located at Fatehgarh Sahib and its building constructed in shape of Sikh religious symbol Khanda. The museum is built by SGPC in the memory of Sikh-warrior Banda Singh Bahadur and would exhibit his history.

topPlaces.name: Aam Khas Bagh
topPlaces.description: Aam Khas Bagh is the remains of a highway-inn. It was divided into two parts - the Aam for public use and the Khas for private use by royalty.

topPlaces.name: Sadna Qasai Mosque
topPlaces.description: The Sadna Qasai Mosque is a former mosque in a ruinous state, located in Sirhind, in the state of Punjab, India. It is listed as a state protected monument.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Manupur
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Manupur took place on 11 March 1748 between the forces of the rising Durrani Empire led by Afghan Emperor Ahmad Shah Durrani and the armies of the Mughal Empire led by Mughal Vizier Qamar ud-Din. It was part of Ahmad Shah's first invasion of India.

topPlaces.name: Sirhind
topPlaces.description: Sirhind is a twin city of Fatehgarh Sahib in Punjab, India. It is hosts the municipal council of Fatehgarh Sahib district.

topPlaces.name: Chandigarh Capital Region
topPlaces.description: Chandigarh Capital Region (CCR) or Chandigarh Metropolitan Region (CMR) is an area, which includes the union territory city of Chandigarh, and its neighboring cities of Mohali, Kharar, Zirakpur, New Chandigarh (in Punjab) and Panchkula, Pinjore, Kalka, Barwala (in Haryana). Chandigarh Administration, Greater Mohali Area Development Authority (GMADA) and Haryana Urban Development Authority (HUDA) are different authorities responsible for development of this region.

topPlaces.name: Bhatt Majra, Fatehgarh Sahib
topPlaces.description: Bhatt Majra is a village in Sirhind mandal, district Fatehgarh Sahib, state Punjab, India. The village is on the world map for organic farming, visited by Prince Charles in 2005.

topPlaces.name: Bassi Pathana
topPlaces.description: Bassi Pathana is a historical city and a municipal council in Fatehgarh Sahib district in the state of Punjab, India. It is well known for being Asia's first market for sewing machines.

items.note: Khanda Museum is a museum located at Fatehgarh Sahib and its building constructed in shape of Sikh religious symbol Khanda. The museum is built by SGPC in the memory of Sikh-warrior Banda Singh Bahadur and would exhibit his history.
Aam Khas Bagh is the remains of a highway-inn. It was divided into two parts - the Aam for public use and the Khas for private use by royalty.
The Sadna Qasai Mosque is a former mosque in a ruinous state, located in Sirhind, in the state of Punjab, India. It is listed as a state protected monument.

The Battle of Manupur took place on 11 March 1748 between the forces of the rising Durrani Empire led by Afghan Emperor Ahmad Shah Durrani and the armies of the Mughal Empire led by Mughal Vizier Qamar ud-Din. It was part of Ahmad Shah's first invasion of India.
Sirhind is a twin city of Fatehgarh Sahib in Punjab, India. It is hosts the municipal council of Fatehgarh Sahib district.

Chandigarh Capital Region (CCR) or Chandigarh Metropolitan Region (CMR) is an area, which includes the union territory city of Chandigarh, and its neighboring cities of Mohali, Kharar, Zirakpur, New Chandigarh (in Punjab) and Panchkula, Pinjore, Kalka, Barwala (in Haryana). Chandigarh Administration, Greater Mohali Area Development Authority (GMADA) and Haryana Urban Development Authority (HUDA) are different authorities responsible for development of this region.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gurdwara Jyoti Sarup?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gurdwara Jyoti Sarup is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gurdwara Jyoti Sarup?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chandigarh International Airport (37 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (39 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gurdwara Jyoti Sarup?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gurdwara Jyoti Sarup at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gurdwara Jyoti Sarup?
faq.a: Stays in Gurdwara Jyoti Sarup start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gurdwara Jyoti Sarup?
faq.a: The top attractions are Khanda Museum, Aam Khas Bagh, Sadna Qasai Mosque, Battle of Manupur, Sirhind.
